_comment: Copy this file to config.json and adjust as needed. config.json is excluded from version control.
_comment_models: Define all AI models you want to use. The key is the name used throughout config.json to reference this model. 'platform' must be 'OpenAI' or 'Claude'. 'model' is the API model identifier sent to the provider.
_comment_current_engine: Default model used for any task not explicitly assigned in model_assignments.

_comment_task_list: STAGE 1: PARSING
stage1.definitions.detect_definition_list  - AI confirmation gate for USLM definition-list sub-unit extraction
STAGE 2: DEFINITION EXTRACTION AND SCOPE RESOLUTION
stage2.definitions.extract_terms           - Extract defined terms from item text
stage2.definitions.extract_definition      - Extract definition for specific term
stage2.definitions.extract_scope           - Extract scope phrase from definition
stage2.definitions.extract_scope_with_indirect - Extract scope for definitions with indirect references
stage2.definitions.resolve_scope           - Resolve scope phrase to document locations
stage2.definitions.evaluate_quality        - Evaluate definition quality
stage2.definitions.retry_extraction        - Retry poor quality definition extraction
stage2.definitions.construct_from_target   - Build definition from target location
stage2.definitions.validate_external       - Validate external reference
stage2.definitions.resolve_indirect        - Resolve indirect reference location
stage2.definitions.detect_elaborative      - Detect elaborative relationship
stage2.definitions.resolve_conflict        - Resolve conflicting definitions
stage2.definitions.review_high_conflict    - Review high-conflict terms for genuine definitions
STAGE 3: SUMMARIZATION
stage3.summary.organizational              - Generate organizational unit summary
stage3.summary.organizational.batch_interim - Interim summary for one batch when org children exceed threshold
stage3.summary.level1                      - Generate level 1 item summary (basic)
stage3.summary.level1_with_references      - Generate level 1 with reference extraction
stage3.summary.level2                      - Generate level 2 item summary (detailed)
stage3.summary.topic_statement             - Generate document topic statement
stage3.summary.table                       - Generate structural summary for large table sub-unit
STAGE 4: QUESTION ANSWERING
qa.relevance.score                         - Score item relevance to question
qa.analysis.analyze_chunk                  - Analyze chunk for question (iterative)
qa.synthesis.cleanup_scratch               - Cleanup scratch document
qa.synthesis.final_answer                  - Generate final narrative answer
CROSS-REFERENCE REGISTRY
registry.resolution.ai                     - AI-assisted resolution for unparseable citations (--ai flag)
USAGE:
If a task is not specified here, it uses the model from 'current_engine'
Example: "stage3.summary.topic_statement": "gpt-5" uses GPT-5 for topic statements
model_assignments._comment_example_assignments: Example model assignments (uncomment and modify as needed):

retry._comment_purpose: Configure how many times to retry with each model and which fallback models to use
retry._comment_max_retries: Number of retries with each model before trying the next fallback (default: 3)
retry._comment_fallback_models: Global fallback models tried when primary model fails (tried in order)
retry._comment_task_specific: Task-specific fallback models override global fallbacks

_comment_log_stem: Base filename for log files written during processing (e.g. 'log' produces 'log_stage2.txt').
_comment_similarity_threshold: Minimum similarity score (0-100) for fuzzy-matching term names during definition scope resolution.
_comment_max_iteration_count: Maximum Stage 2 processing rounds before halting (guards against infinite loops in conflict resolution).

output._comment: directory: where processed documents are stored; supports ~ for home directory. structure: 'per_document' (one subdirectory per source file, recommended) or 'flat' (all files in one directory).

_comment_document_roots: Used by the UI to scan for source documents. Each entry defines a named collection of source files the UI can browse and submit for processing. 'parser' must match a registered parser type (e.g. 'uslm', 'cfr', 'ca_html', 'formex'). 'path' is the directory to scan. 'display_name' is shown in the UI. 'scan_recursive' defaults to true. 'enabled' defaults to true.

document_roots._comment_example: Remove this comment key and add real entries. Example:

_comment_job_queue: Optional. Override the default job queue database path (default: <output.directory>/jobs.db). Useful if you want the queue database on a different volume from processed output.

job_queue._comment: Uncomment 'database' to override the default path.

processing._comment_default_checkpoint_threshold: Number of items to process before writing a checkpoint file. Higher values reduce checkpoint overhead but risk more lost work if interrupted.
processing._comment_default_parse_mode: How to handle multi-chapter documents: 'split_chapters' (one output file per chapter, recommended for large titles) or 'full_document' (single output file).
processing._comment_cumulative_summary_list_max_cha: Stage 3 level-1 summarization maintains a rolling list of prior section summaries as AI context. This caps the total character count of that list; oldest entries are dropped when the cap is exceeded. Increase if you want more context carried forward; decrease to reduce prompt size and cost.
processing._comment_org_summary_batch_threshold: Stage 3 organizational summary: when combined child-section summaries exceed this character count, the call is split into batches with an interim summary per batch and a final synthesis. Prevents oversized prompts for parts with many sections.
processing._comment_level2_tool_use_threshold: Stage 3 level-2 summary: when the front-loaded context block (definitions + table of contents) exceeds this character count, it is replaced with AI-callable lookup tools. The AI requests only the definitions and TOC sections it needs, reducing prompt size.
processing._comment_definition_list_thresholds: Per-parser thresholds for definition-list sub-unit extraction. min_items = minimum number of list entries; min_chars = minimum total text characters. Formex uses 4000 chars — EU legislative articles are concise and a qualifying list rarely exceeds that without being a genuine definitions article. USLM uses 6000 chars because USC sections often have dense preamble text that can push non-definition subsections past the 4000-char mark; the higher bar reduces false positives before the AI confirmation gate fires.

_comment_extract_refs_ai_pass: Run AI resolution pass after Stage 3 (registry/post_stage3.py). Resolves citations with non-standard formatting that regex cannot parse. Uses model assigned to registry.resolution.ai. Default: false (regex-only resolution).
_comment_question_answering_modes: Named Q&A modes selectable at runtime with --mode. Each mode is a profile of settings controlling scoring depth, analysis passes, and scratch-document management. 'default_qa_mode' names the mode used when --mode is not specified.

_comment_mode_keys: scoring_summary_level       - Which summary level to use for relevance scoring: 'summary_1' (faster) or 'summary_2' (more thorough)
org_summary_scoring         - Whether to score organizational units (parts, subparts) in addition to leaf sections
stop_after_scoring          - If true, return scored units without running analysis (quick relevance scan only)
max_analysis_passes         - Maximum number of analysis rounds over the scored unit set
quality_check_phase         - Whether to run a post-analysis quality check pass
analyze_zero_score_sections - Whether to analyze units scored 0 (not relevant) during the quality check pass
scoring_fallback_to_summary_2 - If summary_1 is unavailable for a unit, fall back to summary_2 for scoring
scoring_batch_max_chars     - Maximum combined characters per batch when scoring multiple units in one AI call
scoring_batch_max_items     - Maximum number of units per scoring batch
score_1_gate                - Gate score-1 units (low relevance): skip on first analysis pass, include in later passes only if no better answers found
deduplicate_new_facts       - Run end-of-round deduplication on newly added scratch facts
compact_after_additions     - Run scratch compaction after every N fact additions (0 = no interval compaction)
implicit_reference_detection - Detect implicit cross-references in analyst scratch facts and add referenced units to the analysis queue
